results.name: uses-new-dimensions
results.evidence: Output tables explicitly use C1-C4 (Controllability) and O1-O4 (Observability) and V1-V3 (Value) dimensions. E1-E4 dimensions do not appear anywhere in the output.

results.name: code-injection-not-files
results.evidence: Proposed patches include [HARNESS-INJECT-START/END] markers in source files. Modified src_bootstrap_state.ts contains markers at lines 1759-1770 (config_control injection, 12 lines). Modified src_utils_log.ts contains markers at lines 364-406 (structured_log injection, 43 lines). No .harness/ wrapper scripts are used.

results.name: injection-point-analysis
results.evidence: Output identifies two specific files with line ranges: (1) src/bootstrap/state.ts at '~1758 (end of file)' with rationale about being a central state module, and (2) src/utils/log.ts at '~362 (end of file)' with rationale about being the natural home for structured harness logging. Both include existing idioms noted.

results.name: shows-approval-flow
results.evidence: Output contains a clear two-phase flow: (1) PATCH PROPOSALS section (lines 59-151) showing proposed patches with code diffs, and (2) APPROVAL & INJECTION / INJECT — Applying Patches section (lines 154-183) where patches are actually applied with confirmation output.

results.name: tier-recommendation
results.evidence: Output explicitly states 'Recommended starting tier: Tier 1 (Config Control) — minimal intrusion, foundational capability' at the conclusion of the ASSESSMENT section (line 39).
